trigger_reason: The investigation agent is prioritizing mapping activities to high-level frameworks (MITRE) while neglecting low-level data validation and contextual historical lookups, resulting in 'summary-heavy' but 'evidence-light' reporting.
improvements: Add a mandatory 'Forensic Evidence Proof' section where specific file hashes and memory artifact strings must be explicitly cited to support conclusions.
Transition from binary 'check' status to a 'Evidence Requirement' status: Investigators must provide a specific data point or log ID to mark a checklist item as 'Complete'.

Integrate a 'Negative Confirmation' requirement for persistence checks—the agent must explicitly state which persistence locations (WMI/Run Keys) were scanned and return 'None Found' rather than leaving status as 'Pending'.

checklist.note: Reconstruct full chronological sequence with integrated host-based log events and account behavioral baseline comparison.
Extract all IPs, domains, hashes, usernames, and techniques; must include IP/ASN reputation scores for each remote host.
Map to MITRE ATT&CK; must link each stage to specific evidence IDs (log entries/memory artifacts).
Evidence-based check of Run keys, scheduled tasks, startup items, and WMI. Must document findings for all locations.
Mandatory extraction of volatile memory artifacts and script hashes (e.g., PowerShell script block logs) to validate execution claims.
Assess data volume, destination geo-location, and sensitivity. Link to network flow logs.
